verdict.answer_first: A states the prescription immediately: "start with a Postgres table"; B also starts with "use a DB table" but adds more conditions in the first sentence.
verdict.decisiveness: A is more decisive: "DB table — the right default" and "pg-boss / graphile-worker"; B surveys more: "DB table if... BullMQ... cloud queue..."
verdict.fabrication: both add unverified thresholds: A "a few hundred jobs/sec"; B "a few hundred jobs/sec" and "thousands of jobs/sec"
verdict.actionability: A is easier to execute because it says "via a library, not hand-rolled" and names "pg-boss or graphile-worker"; B says DB table but describes hand-building pieces.
